text.title: Slash-Command Surface for PR Review Bots Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules pinning the auto-trigger policy, slash-command surface, and skip path for review bots.

rule.statement: Bot config MUST set `auto_describe = true` on pull_request `opened`, `reopened`, `ready_for_review`, `synchronize` events.

rationale.source: Qodo Merge usage guide + CodeRabbit production studies

rule.rationale: Auto-describe covers the always-useful 80% — title and walkthrough are stale-free.

rule.statement: Bot config MUST set `auto_review = true` on the same events as auto_describe.
rule.rationale: Inline issues posted as the human review starts saves the human's first pass.

rule.statement: Bot config MUST set `auto_improve = false`; `/improve` MUST be opt-in via explicit PR comment.
rule.rationale: Auto-running improve emits 5–15 suggestion patches per PR and trains reviewers to ignore the bot.

rule.statement: Workflow MUST listen for the `issue_comment.created` event so `/describe`, `/review`, `/improve`, `/ask` posted as PR comments dispatch the matching bot action.
rule.rationale: Without `issue_comment` events the slash commands are decoration; the bot cannot react to PR-comment requests.

rule.statement: Auto-describe MUST update only the marker-delimited block (`AUTO-DESCRIBE-START`/`END`); human-authored content outside the block MUST survive re-runs.
rule.rationale: Without idempotent blocks, every re-run overwrites human notes and trust collapses.

rule.statement: Workflow MUST gate on `github.event.sender.type != 'Bot'` so bot-on-bot comment cascades cannot trigger another bot run.
rule.rationale: Otherwise the bot loops on its own comments and burns API quota.

rule.statement: If repo is single-author, tiny-PR-only, or has a "no third-party app + no self-host" policy, STOP.
rule.rationale: Skip applies to repos where the bot's overhead exceeds its value.
